questions.question: What is a political party?
questions.answer: A political party is a group of people who come together to contest elections and hold power in government.

questions.question: Name one component of a political party.
What is the main aim of political parties?
questions.answer: To gain political power and form government.

questions.question: Which institution registers political parties in India?
Which system is followed in India?
Name a country with a two-party system.
Name a country with a one-party system.
What is a coalition government?
questions.answer: A government formed by an alliance of several political parties.

questions.question: Which party forms the government after elections?
questions.answer: The party that wins the majority of seats.

questions.question: What role do opposition parties play?
Name one national party in India.
Name one regional party in India.
What are state parties?
questions.answer: Political parties that operate mainly within a state.

questions.question: What is internal democracy?
questions.answer: Participation of party members in decision making.

questions.question: What is dynastic succession?
What is money power in politics?
questions.answer: Use of money to influence elections.

questions.question: Which law prevents representatives from changing parties?
Why is transparency important in elections?
questions.answer: It helps voters know candidates’ backgrounds.

questions.question: How do political parties shape public opinion?
questions.answer: By raising issues and conducting campaigns.

questions.question: What is the role of media in politics?
questions.answer: Media informs citizens about political activities.

questions.question: What is the role of citizens in political reforms?
questions.answer: Citizens demand reforms and vote responsibly.

questions.question: What do political parties present during elections?
Which system allows many parties to compete in elections?
Which factor affects fairness of elections due to wealth?
Why are political parties important in democracy?
questions.answer: They organize elections and form governments.
